<p></p><h1>LISTEN TO THIS:</h1><p></p>
</div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=17992484, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-17992484">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>They say email is dead?</p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">Here's the truth:</span></p><p>91% of people check their email daily.</p><p>66% of people will buy something online because of an email they received. (Email makes more sales than every other source of marketing available.)</p><p>Also...</p><p>While Facebook has 3 billion users... Instagram has 2 billion users... And Tiktok has 1.5 users...</p><p>Email has 5 billion users!</p><p>Let THAT sink in.</p><p>And what makes the social media numbers worse is that Facebook and Instagram only lets 5% of your followers see what you post. Unless, of course you pay them (by "boosting" posts etc.).</p><p>But with email... YOU control it.</p><p>YOU own it.</p><h2><strong>Email is the mothership.</strong></h2><p>Getting email only sucks when it's email you don't want.</p><p>And these days, that's most of it.</p><p>So your job as an artist or brand is to write emails that "your people" want.</p><p>"But Bronques... People don't even <em>open</em> my emails..."</p><p><em>That's because your emails aren't fun. They’re not inspiring. </em></p><p>You have to write epic emails. You need to inspire your audience. Inspire them with your creativity. Inspire them with your knowledge. Inspire them with your cute products. But never be "middle of the road" with anything you do. This is your life's work! Share your vision. Amplify the details. Listen, you can be as shy as you want to be in real life... but email is no place to hold back! They already love you. That's why they signed up. You can't afford to give them less than 100%. </p><p>Try this... Check your inbox right now.</p><p>I bet 90% of it is just junk that you delete, delete, delete... BUT... There's also a very small percentage of emails that you're happy to get. Some that put a smile on your face or maybe inspire you. You may even have a special folder for those so you can read them again later. </p><p>What YOU need to start doing is: stop writing emails that are designed for people's “delete pile”... and creating those that have a place in their “special folder”.</p><p>People open emails because of the way you made them feel the last time they opened one from you.</p><p>Everything you see me do on instagram, facebook, twitter or snapchat started with an email. No joke.</p><h2><strong>Email is the mothership.</strong></h2><p>"But Bronques... no one READS anymore."</p><p>Bro, people read. But only if you write about something they care about.</p><p><em>This is not a theory. I tested it.</em></p><p>Back in September 2018 I did a little experiment. I sent out an email to my list every single day. I called it "30 emails in 30 days". I went against everything that people usually say... "People will get mad." "They'll unsubscribe." "You'll burn people out.". </p><p>Guess what? </p><p>They were right. All of those things DID happen. </p><p><strong>I got 400 unsubscribes in 30 days</strong></p><p>But I also got 1,100 NEW subscribers in that same time frame when I shared one of those emails on social with an opt-in link (link to join my list). </p><p>And you know what also happened? </p><p>My open rates went up! </p><p>And people started writing me back to comment and share their own stories. </p><p>I developed relationships with the people on my list (relationships that continue to this day). </p><p>My "email marketing" became a great source for getting feedback and testing ideas.</p><p>And throughout that whole period I was posting LESS on Instagram... but guess what? My Instagram engagement, comments and DMs INCREASED. Why? By the time my followers were seeing photos on my social media the truest fans already knew the backstory through email. They all had this shared BTS knowledge. All my social media platforms became more meaningful because of this new two-way communication that I had now established within my email army. </p><p><em>So now I love it when people 'unsubscribe'.</em></p><p>I know that those people are not "my people".</p><p>My open rate is 46% now.</p><p>Before I started my "emailing every day" experiment, my open rate was 19%.</p><p>It went UP after I started bombarding people's inbox every day. Counter-intuitive, right? </p><p>The truth is... People never get tired of hearing from you if you're always addressing their needs. Their "needs" is your message. And those "people" are your psychographic. That's what they signed up for.</p><p>"But Bronques," you stubbornly wanna protest, "I only have 20 people on my email list!" </p><p>I don't care if you have 8 people on your email list and only 3 opens.</p><h2><strong>Email is the mothership.</strong></h2><p>Here's the strategy.</p><p>Make email your "pillar content".</p><p>Those emails you force yourself to write today will become an endless sources of writing for you to draw from later. The sheer volume of words that you can now pull from when you need to write captions, describe your merch, create compelling ads or find the topic for a new viral video... will leave you stress-free when you're in a pinch.</p><p>But the best part is... </p><p>That "refurbished writing" can also make you lots of money!</p><p>This is not a theory. I tested that too.</p><p>I turned my most popular emails into ads, and they all had a relevance score of 10. Every single one. Now if you're not a Facebook ads person let me explain: a high “relevance score” means that Facebook notices that the community likes what you're showing them. Your ads end up <strong>costing you less</strong> AND converting more because the algorithm knows exactly who to show them to. </p><p>But the most undeniable business reason to nurture and grow your email list is this: <em>Facebook ads re-targetting.</em> (Sorry, I'm gonna get nerdy here...) When you create a "custom audience" with your email list... you can lower the CPA down to a 1/4 of the price. And if you had segmented and purged your list for the last 3 years you could have gotten it even lower! You get to laugh all the way to the bank while all your competition whines about the rising costs of ads. </p><p>I see so many great artists leaving "email marketing" money on the table. </p><p>They have a message and a spellbound audience, but they don't ever talk to them. </p><p>WTF??</p><p>They get email opt-ins daily but they have no automated "welcome sequences"!</p><p>WTF??</p><p>I mean... even a badly written automated welcome email is more polite than NO welcome email. </p><p>It reminds me of the first scene of the movie 'The Godfather'. The part where Marlon Brando says: "You come to see me and ask me to kill a man but you never once called to say hello?"</p><p>That's how most people behave with email. They ignore their fans until they have a new video to promote, or a new song, or some merch to sell you. </p><blockquote><strong>"Stop treating your list like a booty call." Yara Golden</strong></blockquote><p><em>Dig your well before you're thirsty.</em></p><p>Like... Maybe you should start connecting with your fans BEFORE you start asking them for money and favors?</p><p>The internet has taught us to be rude. You don't have to follow that trend. (#thequietrevolution btw)</p><p>When you start to use your values and principles to navigate social media instead of copying the bad behavior that you see everywhere, your whole experience online will change for the better.</p><p>I love movies. I love analogies. So, here's one last one.</p><p>One of my favorite scenes in Good Will Hunting is when Matt Damon is telling his therapist (Robin Williams) about his awesome date with a girl. When the therapist asks "Are you gonna call her?" He says "No, I don't want to ruin it. She's so perfect right now." The therapist cuts him up with: "Maybe you don't wanna call her because... YOU'RE so perfect right now..."</p><p><em>That's how artists treat their fans. Afraid of offending. Afraid of unsubscribes. So they go on ignoring all their people until they want something. They're so afraid to offend... that they act indifferently. </em></p><h2><strong>Email is The Mothership.</strong></h2><p>While everyone else is stressing out over social media, a few of us are sharing exclusive photos and crazy stories with our fans in a way that’s soooo old school it’s become shockingly... new!</p><p>All of the power today is in words.</p><p>So now that you and I are on the same page...</p><p>I'm gonna show you how to set it up for yourself:</p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=32045688, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-32045688">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1><strong>TAKE ACTION:</strong></h1><h1><br class="softbreak"></h1><h1><strong>CHOOSE AN EMAIL PROVIDER</strong></h1><p>They're all the same. It doesn't matter which one you pick.</p><p>Mailchimp is popular and is better for "designed" emails. (free until 2k followers)</p><p><a href="https://kit.com/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Kit</a> is artist friendly and better for mostly text emails (like the one's I send). (free until 500 followers)</p><p>If you're thinking of turning your words into a business (writers only) then maybe you should look at Substack too.</p><p>ActiveCampaign is the one I use now because I'm managing so many artist students, but I've used the other 2 before my business grew.</p><p>I'd encourage you to use <a href="https://kit.com/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Kit.com</a> because it keeps you focused on sending a super-basic email that looks like emails you'd send on Gmail to your friends. That's all you need. Leave fancy emails to evil "marketers" who don't even get their emails opened-up anyways.<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>CREATE A "WELCOME EMAIL" </strong></h1><p>A "welcome email" is an email that goes out to people AS SOON AS THEY SIGN UP TO YOUR LIST.</p><p>This is important.</p><p>Don't collect emails. And falsely convince yourself that you will write them soon.</p><p>No, rig your system so that as soon as you add someone's name, or they subscribe on your landing page, they<strong> immediately </strong>get an email from you telling them what to expect etc... Make it fun.</p><p>But, seriously though.</p><p>Don't overthink this step please.</p><p>Just write this welcome email like you're writing to a friend. 'Cause, um... You are!'</p><p><em>Remember this sentence when you're not sure what to write:</em></p><p><em>"Tell the truth."</em></p><p>Whenever we struggle with words it's because we're trying to "present a fake self to people". This isn't Instagram. You don't have to do that anymore. This is the platform where it pays to be real.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>CREATE A "LANDING PAGE"</strong></h1><p>This is literally all you need to get in the game:</p><p>A "landing page" is a one page "website" that tells people what you're about (keep it short - Mailchimp and ConvertKit both have simple easy to design landing pages you can edit).</p><p><em>The difference between a website and a landing page is focused attention.</em></p><p>Websites are outdated online brochures with distracting links to your socials, and your "about page" (gross)...</p><p>But with a landing page you help your audience focus on ONE thing. Just like a storyteller. You guide them to the next part of the journey:</p><p>Invite the visitor to join your email list. Tell them exactly what to expect from your emails.</p><p>Later, you can grow this into a full-fledged old school website if you want. But for now, make gathering emails the centerpiece of your online presence.</p><p>(Btw, notice that I put this step AFTER the "write your welcome email" step? That's 'cause you don't wanna have a landing page where people can sign up before they can get an automatic email reply. So many people get this wrong. I REPEAT. It's not about blindly collecting emails! It's about communicating. If you get someone's email they have to hear from you right away or they'll forget they even signed up...)</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>LINK IN BIO</strong></h1><p>Now's the time to put your IG to good use.</p><p>Use the LINK IN BIO to send people to your "one page website" so they can join your list. Stop using LINKTREE to give people a million choices. Give them only one option: to get on your email list.</p><p>Once they're on your list, you can share new art with them, sell merch, tell them stories. Whatever you want. Instead of the way you have it right now where they only go check out your stuff ONCE.</p><p>Now, the link for your email list that your email provider gives you may be too ugly, or too long. If you have $$ you can invest in your own custom URL. But the truth is there's an even better option:</p><p>Use a service like <a href="https://bitly.com/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">bitly.com</a> to create a cute new custom URL that people will wanna click.</p><p>The advantage is that now you'll be able to QUICKLY see how many people click on your "link in bio" every day. So fucking useful especially after you promote it on IG story so you can see how well your efforts are doing. There are longer ways to do this using "IG insights" and google analytics, but trust me you don't wanna go there.</p><p>And if you notice... I have a custom URL and I still use bitly.</p><p>Once you've set all that up, give yourself a pat on the back because:</p><p>Just like that... Overnight... Your IG full of selfies is more useful than it was yesterday.</p><p>You're starting to take your career more seriously.</p><p>I applaud you.</p><p>Treat yourself to desert. Take a shot of tequila for me (I can't drink right now -- I'm in full God Momentum).</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>DECIDE HOW OFTEN YOU'LL BE COMMUNICATING</strong></h1><p>...and then stick to it.</p><p>It doesn't matter if you're only writing to 8 people.</p><p>Write about things you both care about.</p><p>Get them used to hearing from you.</p><p>And use some (or all) of the words you write-- as captions on Instagram. That way you're getting even more use out of all this creativity.</p><p>"Pillar content" remember?</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>NEW SUBSCRIBERS</strong></h1><p>Wait a few days after they've received the "welcome email", and then make contact with them by sending a personal non-automated email.</p><p>It doesn't have to be long. You're just touching base and giving that "human touch". I love technology just as much as everyone else, but I think that the marriage of digital AND non-digital is the future for creative brands and artists.</p><p>You won't be able to do this when you're bigger, but do it when you're small. That's how you turn "nothing" into "something"</p><p>Besides, there's a "business" benefit to it also:</p><p>Sometimes subscription based emails get filtered into junk folders by faulty algorithms. This gives you a chance to show how much you value their interest in what you're doing AND to remind them to add your address to their address book so that you never end up in junk. (This is called 'whitelisting'.)</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>GROW. THRIVE.</strong></h1><p>When you meet people at parties...</p><p>Or on the street...</p><p>Or at dinner...</p><p>Get their email (and permission).</p><p>Add them to your growing list of people that hear from you regularly.</p><p><em>I remember doing this when I started taking pictures.</em></p><p>And you know what's crazy?</p><p>I got a job offer or an invitation to something <em>almost every time</em> my "newsletter" went out.</p><p>These were email's I had gathered from everyone I would meet (and you know I meet tons of people).</p><p>So as soon as someone in the industry got a new email from me, it's like they suddenly remembered me-- and how I might be perfect for the thing that they're working on.</p><p>You've heard the expression "Out of sight, out of mind."</p><p><em>Well, email keeps you always in plain sight, and within mind.</em></p><h2>If you have an email list (or you're planning to start one), email me right now and I'll send you more of my special 'ART OF EMAIL' secrets.</h2><p></p>
</div>
